description: Return your pipeline — every project you're watching, with stage, tags, raise size, and last-updated timestamp. Optionally filter by stage or tag. (requires API key)

labels.name: list_intros
labels.description: List the intro threads you're a party to (sent as an investor or received as a founder). Each entry includes the counterparty, message count, last-message preview, and whether you have unread messages. Optionally filter by your role or unread-only. (requires API key)

labels.name: read_intro_thread
labels.description: Read all messages in a specific intro thread you're a party to. Marks the thread as read for you. (requires API key)

labels.name: send_intro_message
labels.description: Append a message to an existing intro thread you're a party to. The other party gets an email notification linking back to the thread. Use this to follow up after request_intro or to keep a conversation moving. (requires API key)

labels.name: submit_commitment
labels.description: Record an investor's intent-to-fund a project. Without terms it starts at 'soft_circled'; attach a terms object and it starts at 'term_sheet'. The founder still has to acknowledge — a commitment doesn't count as 'committed' (and doesn't show in the public round progress) until both sides have acked.

labels.name: acknowledge_commitment
labels.description: Acknowledge a commitment as the founder or the investor. When both sides have acknowledged, status auto-promotes from soft_circled/term_sheet to 'committed' and the amount is added to the project's public round progress. Calling twice from the same side is a no-op. (requires API key)

labels.name: update_commitment_status
labels.description: Change a commitment's status. Role-gated: only the investor can 'withdraw' a commitment, only the founder can mark it 'funded' (after the wire lands). Either side can move between soft_circled/term_sheet/committed. (requires API key)

labels.name: list_commitments
labels.description: List commitments where you're either the investor or the founder. Filter by project slug, your role, or status. (requires API key)

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_intros, read_intro_thread · Private data: request_intro, watch_project, update_watch, list_watches, list_intros, read_intro_thread, send_intro_message, submit_commitment, acknowledge_commitment, update_commitment_status, list_commitments · Public sink: request_intro, send_intro_message, submit_commitment.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_intros, read_intro_thread · Private data: request_intro, watch_project, update_watch, list_watches, list_intros, read_intro_thread, send_intro_message, submit_commitment, acknowledge_commitment, update_commitment_status, list_commitments · Public sink: request_intro, send_intro_message, submit_commitment

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
